zh.Bywell, Northumberland, England: 英國英格蘭諾圖波藍德白維爾
zh.lifeSummary: 當William Lawson於1400年出生在英國諾圖波藍德白維爾時，他的父親John Lawson of Bywell50歲，母親Elizabeth Kemp40歲。他於1425年之前與Agnes Cramlington結婚。他們至少是7個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1480年5月27日在英國諾圖波藍德可拉苗林頓享年80歲。

zh-hans.Bywell, Northumberland, England: 英国英格兰諾圖波藍德白維爾
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当William Lawson于1400年出生在英国諾圖波藍德白維爾时，他的父亲John Lawson of Bywell50岁，母亲Elizabeth Kemp40岁。他于1425年之前与Agnes Cramlington结婚。他们至少是7个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他去世于1480年5月27日在英国諾圖波藍德可拉苗林頓享年80岁。

nameMeanings.name: Lawson
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish and northern English: patronymic ‘son of Lawrence’; see   Law   1. It is also found in Ireland since the 17th century.
Americanized form of Swedish   Larsson   and Danish, Norwegian or North German   Larsen  .

variants.name: Law
meanings: Scottish and northern English: from a Middle English personal name Law(e), a short form of Lawrence .

Scottish and northern English: topographic name for someone who lived near a hill, northern Middle English law (from Old English hlāw, which generally became low in the south and law in the north). Compare Lowe and Lew .
Chinese: variant Romanization of the surname 羅, based on its Cantonese pronunciation, see   Luo   1.

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When William Lawson was born in 1400, in Bywell, Northumberland, England, his father, John Lawson of Bywell, was 50 and his mother, Elizabeth Kemp, was 40. He married Agnes Cramlington before 1425. They were the parents of at least 7 sons and 1 daughter. He died on 27 May 1480, in Cramlington, Northumberland, England, at the age of 80.

ko.lifeSummary: William Lawson(이)가 1400년 영국 노르스벌랜드 비웰에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 John Lawson of Bywell(은)는 50세였고, 그의 어머니 Elizabeth Kemp(은)는 40세였다. 그는 1425년 이전 Agnes Cramlington(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 일곱 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1480년 5월 27일 영국 노르스벌랜드 크램링턴에서 80세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда William Lawson родился в 1400 г. в местности бивелл, нортумбирланд, Англия, его отцу по имени John Lawson of Bywell было 50 лет, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth Kemp, 40. Он женился на женщине по имени Agnes Cramlington перед 1425 г. У них было по крайней мере 7 сыновей и 1 дочь. Он умер 27 мая 1480 г. в местности крамлингтон, нортумбирланд, Англия в возрасте 80 лет.
